header.title: Binary Search
header.description: An efficient search algorithm that finds a target in a sorted array by repeatedly dividing the search space in half.

howItWorks.title: How it works
items: Prerequisite: array must be sorted in ascending order.
Start with left = 0 and right = n - 1.
Compute mid = (left + right) / 2 and compare array[mid] with target.
If equal, return mid. If smaller, search right half. If larger, search left half.
Repeat until found or left > right.

algorithmSteps.title: Step 1
algorithmSteps.text: Start with entire sorted array. Initialize left=0, right=n-1.

algorithmSteps.title: Step 4
algorithmSteps.text: If array[mid] equals target — found! Return mid.

algorithmSteps.title: Step 5
algorithmSteps.text: If array[mid] > target — search left half (right = mid - 1).

algorithmSteps.title: Step 6
algorithmSteps.text: If array[mid] < target — search right half (left = mid + 1).

algorithmSteps.title: Step 7
algorithmSteps.text: If left > right — target not found, return -1.

footerAlerts.title: Why Binary Search is powerful
items: O(log n) time — each step eliminates half the remaining elements.
Only ~30 comparisons for a billion elements.
Foundation for answer-space search and many interview patterns.

footerAlerts.title: Real-world applications
items: Dictionary / catalog search on sorted data.

footerAlerts.title: Prerequisites
items: Needs random access (arrays, not linked lists).

shortcuts: ← previous step · → next step · Space play/pause · R reset
